key_insight: SoftVTBench shows that deformable-object manipulation should be judged not only by task success, but also by physical safety metrics like no-drop stability and bounded deformation.
engineering_relevance: This benchmark helps robotic manipulation researchers evaluate policies for real-world soft material handling where excessive deformation or slipping can cause damage or failure.
method: Built in Isaac Sim with FEM-simulated deformable objects and multi-modal inputs: multi-view RGB, RGB tactile marker motion, proprioception, and language instructions.
Defined four matched task suites across deformable/rigid objects and object/spatial variation axes.
Introduced two evaluation metrics: Goal Success and Safety Success, with safety requiring no drop and peak deformation below an object-specific threshold from hidden FEM states.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that success-only evaluation overstates performance, and adding tactile sensing raises Safety Success from 21.4% to 35.6% on object-centric deformable tasks while keeping Goal Success comparable.
watch_item: Safety labels depend on privileged FEM states and calibrated deformation thresholds, so benchmark results may not transfer directly to hardware without careful real-world threshold mapping.
paper_title: SoftVTBench: A Safety-Aware Visuo-Tactile Benchmark for Physically Constrained Robotic Manipulation of Deformable Objects
